team.name: cybersecurity
team.header: Team dedicated to maintaining data and system security for Elexion Automotive
team.purpose: Implement and maintain cybersecurity measures, conduct penetration testing and vulnerability assessments, stay updated on emerging threats and technologies

team.name: it
team.header: Team responsible for all aspects of information technology at Elexion Automotive
team.purpose: Manage IT infrastructure, software, and hardware assets, ensure smooth operation of systems, provide IT support to employees, maintain data security

channel.name: avoidable_attacks
channel.header: Discuss and review cybersecurity attacks that were deemed avoidable
channel.purpose: Share insights and statistics on avoidable cybersecurity attacks, discuss strategies to prevent future avoidable attacks

channel.name: cybersecurity_updates
channel.header: Share updates and news related to cybersecurity
channel.purpose: Stay informed about the latest cybersecurity threats, technologies, and industry best practices

post.message: Hello team, remember we've got a meeting scheduled tomorrow at 2pm to discuss the implementation of the new software update across all departments. Let's ensure everyone is prepared.
Guys, let's make sure we're all on the same page about the presentation for next week's cybersecurity conference. I'll be sharing the final draft by EOD tomorrow.
I've received feedback from the customer support team about some software glitches they've been facing. We need to address this issue ASAP.
Can we brainstorm ideas for the upcoming cybersecurity awareness training? I think it would be beneficial if we include real-life examples to make it more engaging.
We need to finalize the IT budget for the next quarter by the end of this week. Please submit your estimates and requirements as soon as possible.
Let's review our current cybersecurity policies and suggest any necessary updates during our meeting next week. Remember, continuous improvement is key.
Has everyone completed the mandatory software training? Remember, it's essential for everyone to be updated with the latest tools and technologies.
There's a cybersecurity networking event next month that might be of interest. Anyone keen on joining should let me know so we can arrange the passes.
Hello team, I have completed the analysis of the cybersecurity attacks we faced in 2019. From our records, we encountered a total of 500 attacks during that year.
Thanks for the update, Ethan. It would be interesting to know how many of these attacks could have been avoided with current best practices and preventive measures in place.

Great question, John. Upon further analysis, we found out that approximately 440 of those attacks were avoidable if best practices had been followed. This represents about 88% of the total attacks we faced in 2019.

Just a quick update for the IT team. Our findings show that 88% of the cybersecurity attacks we faced in 2019 were avoidable. We need to ensure that we adhere to best practices to avoid such vulnerabilities in the future.
Thanks for sharing this, Ethan. It's a stark reminder of the importance of following security protocols. Let's all work together to reduce this number in the future.
